title: China-to-Oman EV Charger Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: 50 Hz Electrical Configuration and Nameplate Settings
rows.target_requirement: Oman public electricity supply uses 50 Hz system assumptions. EV chargers intended for Oman should have input frequency, voltage range, protection settings, thermal ratings, EMC behavior, metering, and installation instructions aligned with the local supply and the project distributor's connection conditions.
target_standards: Oman distributor service-connection and distribution-code requirements applicable to the site
IEC 61851-1 input supply and safety requirements
IEC 61000 series EMC and power-quality references where specified
rows.china_equivalent: China also uses 50 Hz supply assumptions for ordinary grid-connected chargers, so frequency alone is usually not the largest gap. The remaining issues are voltage tolerance, earthing arrangement, protective-device coordination, labeling, EMC and harmonic evidence, and whether the factory default settings match the Oman site.
china_standards: China 50 Hz grid supply assumptions for EVSE
rows.gap: Confirm the actual Oman site supply voltage, phase configuration, short-circuit level, earthing system, ambient temperature, derating assumptions, upstream protection, and metering layout. Update labels, manuals, firmware limits, and commissioning checklists so a nominal 50 Hz match does not hide site-specific electrical gaps.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where required by Oman distributor connection conditions, electrical-installation approval, project specification, or equipment safety documentation
mandatory_status.cn: 50 Hz operation is standard domestic design context, but China settings and labels do not replace Oman site verification

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] China and Oman both use 50 Hz assumptions, but Oman projects still need site-specific voltage, earthing, protection, EMC, metering, and commissioning checks.

rows.requirement: China GB/T 18487 / GB/T 20234 Baseline vs Oman Evidence Package
rows.target_requirement: An Oman evidence package for imported EV chargers should be built around the actual destination use case: DGSM or customs scope, IEC 61851 safety evidence where requested, IEC 62196 connector choice where requested, distributor grid connection, local labels and manuals, installation method statements, commissioning records, warranty and maintenance obligations, and operator cybersecurity or payment-system requirements where relevant.
target_standards: DGSM and Oman customs scope documentation
IEC 61851 series where specified
IEC 62196 series where specified
APSR-regulated utility and licensed distributor connection requirements
Project tender, fleet, landlord, or charge-point-operator requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China baseline files commonly include GB/T 18487.1-2023 for conductive charging systems, GB/T 20234.1-2023 for general connection-device requirements, GB/T 20234.2-2015 for AC charging interfaces, GB/T 20234.3-2023 for DC charging interfaces, GB/T 27930-2023 for off-board charger to BMS communication, domestic EMC or safety reports, Chinese labels, factory inspection records, and local grid acceptance. These are useful source documents but should be translated, mapped, and supplemented for Oman.
rows.gap: Create an Oman-facing dossier rather than forwarding the China domestic file unchanged. The dossier should include an English clause bridge, connector and communication configuration, nameplate and manual set, import documents, utility drawings, installer responsibilities, commissioning test forms, and a clear statement of what remains project-specific or unverified.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory content depends on Oman regulation, DGSM or customs scope, distributor connection process, tender, contract, and site approval
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T evidence is the China domestic baseline and must be mapped before being used as Oman-facing evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T 18487.1-2023, GB/T 20234.1-2023, GB/T 20234.2-2015, GB/T 20234.3-2023, and GB/T 27930-2023 reports are starting evidence, not an Oman approval. Translate, map, and supplement them against the Oman project route before shipment.

rows.target_requirement: Oman projects may specify IEC 62196 connector families, including Type 2 for AC charging or CCS Combo 2 for DC charging, through utility requirements, procurement documents, site engineering, fleet requirements, or charging-network interoperability rules. A single mandatory national public-charger connector rule should not be assumed unless a binding Oman instrument or project document says so.
target_standards: IEC 62196-1 general requirements
IEC 62196-2 Type 2 AC connector requirements where specified
IEC 62196-3 CCS Combo 2 DC connector requirements where specified
Project connector and interoperability specifications
rows.china_equivalent: China chargers and vehicles commonly use GB/T 20234 connector interfaces, including GB/T 20234.1-2023 for general requirements, GB/T 20234.2-2015 for AC charging interfaces, and GB/T 20234.3-2023 for DC charging interfaces. GB/T AC and DC interfaces are not automatically interoperable with IEC 62196 Type 2 or CCS Combo 2 interfaces, so connector hardware, cable assemblies, communication, locking, labeling, and user instructions may need Oman-specific configuration.
china_standards: GB/T 27930-2023 for China DC charging communication
rows.gap: Do not rely on a China GB/T plug drawing as proof of IEC 62196 interoperability. Confirm the Oman vehicle population or fleet inlet type, public-network connector rule if any, AC or DC communication route, cable ratings, locking behavior, temperature-rise evidence, labeling, and spare-parts plan before shipment.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary or project-specific unless mandated by Oman regulation, tender, concession, public-network rule, fleet contract, utility condition, or another binding instrument
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T connector configuration is commonly expected for China-market equipment and infrastructure; mandatory status depends on the applicable Chinese product, procurement, or project route

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T 20234.1-2023, GB/T 20234.2-2015, GB/T 20234.3-2023, and IEC 62196 connector evidence should be treated as different interface-family evidence unless the exact Oman project documents accept the China connector configuration.

rows.requirement: DGSM Standards and Oman Market Scope Check
rows.target_requirement: Oman's Directorate General for Standards and Metrology is the national standards and conformity authority under the Ministry of Commerce, Industry and Investment Promotion. Oman Ministerial Decree 190/2021 issued the Technical Regulation for the Conformity System under MoCIIP, but the exact EV-charger product scope still needs confirmation. For EV chargers, exporters should confirm whether the exact product, cable assembly, connector, meter, communication module, label, or low-voltage electrical equipment scope is covered by a current Oman standard, Gulf technical regulation, DGSM conformity route, customs control, or buyer tender before shipment.
target_standards: DGSM standards and conformity-assessment services
Oman Ministerial Decree 190/2021: Technical Regulation for the Conformity System (MoCIIP)
Oman technical regulations applicable by product scope
GCC or Gulf technical regulations adopted or applied in Oman where relevant
Project tender, importer, and customs documentation requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China-market charger files usually include GB/T 18487, GB/T 20234, GB/T 27930, Chinese labels, factory inspection records, and domestic test reports. These materials help technical review but do not establish that DGSM, Oman customs, an importer, or a project owner has accepted the product scope or documentation route.
rows.gap: Treat DGSM scope confirmation as an entry task. Prepare product classification, HS code, nameplate ratings, connector type, installation use case, labels, Arabic or English manuals where required, and existing GB/T or IEC reports, then verify whether Oman requires a specific conformity document, local importer filing, or project-level acceptance.

mandatory_status.target: Scope-dependent: mandatory where an Oman technical regulation, DGSM conformity route, Gulf regulation, customs control, tender, or importer requirement applies
mandatory_status.cn: China GB/T documentation is domestic technical evidence and does not by itself establish Oman market acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not assume that a China GB/T charger file is sufficient for Oman until the DGSM, customs, importer, Ministerial Decree 190/2021, and project-documentation route has been checked.

source.publisher: Ministry of Commerce, Industry and Investment Promotion, Oman

rows.target_requirement: IEC 61851-1 is the international reference for EV conductive charging systems, including EV supply equipment operating characteristics, the EV-to-EVSE connection, charging modes, and electrical safety requirements. In Oman, IEC 61851 evidence may be requested by DGSM-related documentation, utilities, consultants, tenders, fleet buyers, insurers, or site engineers even where a single national public-charger type-approval route is not identified for the project.
target_standards: IEC 61851-21-2 for EMC of off-board EV charging systems where applicable
IEC 61851-23 for DC EV charging stations where applicable
IEC 61851-24 for DC charging digital communication where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China EV charger files commonly cite GB/T 18487.1-2023 for conductive charging systems, with GB/T 20234.1-2023, GB/T 20234.2-2015, GB/T 20234.3-2023, and GB/T 27930-2023 often appearing in the connector and DC-communication evidence set. The GB/T evidence can support technical review, but it is not automatically a substitute for IEC 61851 clause mapping, English documentation, Oman installation review, or buyer-specified IEC reports.
rows.gap: Prepare a clause-by-clause bridge from GB/T 18487.1 test reports, EVSE ratings, protection functions, RCD or residual-current strategy, isolation monitoring where relevant, manuals, markings, and installation instructions to the requested IEC 61851 clauses. Confirm the edition and whether the requirement is regulatory, utility-driven, tender-driven, insurer-driven, or voluntary before presenting it as mandatory.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary or project-specific unless incorporated by Oman regulation, DGSM requirement, utility condition, tender, contract, insurer requirement, or electrical-installation approval
mandatory_status.cn: Recommended national standard evidence unless made mandatory by Chinese procurement, project approval, local inspection, or another binding instrument

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T 18487.1-2023 evidence should be mapped to IEC 61851 when Oman project documents, DGSM-related review, or utility engineers ask for IEC-style EVSE safety evidence.

rows.requirement: Nama / Distributor Grid Connection and APSR-Regulated Electricity Sector
rows.target_requirement: Oman electricity-sector activities are regulated by the Authority for Public Services Regulation, including the Electric Vehicle Charging Activity Regulation cited as APSR Decision 15/2023. Connection of EV chargers to a distribution network is normally handled through the relevant licensed distribution company, including Nama group entities where applicable. Charger projects may need site-specific review for load capacity, service connection, metering, protection coordination, earthing, harmonics, power quality, civil works, commissioning, and any charge-point-operator or landlord requirements.
target_standards: APSR-regulated electricity-sector licensing and distribution framework
APSR Decision 15/2023: Electric Vehicle Charging Activity Regulation
Nama Electricity Distribution or other licensed distributor connection procedures where applicable
Oman distribution-code, wiring, metering, and service-connection requirements applicable to the site
Project owner and charge-point-operator commissioning requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic acceptance commonly relies on GB/T 18487 design evidence, GB/T 20234 connectors, GB/T 27930 communication for DC systems, and local grid or project acceptance. These records do not replace Oman distributor connection review, APSR-regulated utility practice, site electrical approval, or local commissioning.
rows.gap: Prepare single-line diagrams, maximum-demand and diversity calculations, charger nameplate ratings, harmonic and EMC data, protection settings, earthing design, metering arrangement, load-management logic, network communication details, installation method statements, commissioning records, and local installer credentials. Oman grid acceptance is site-specific and cannot be proven solely by factory product certification.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected installations where the licensed distributor, Nama entity, APSR-regulated utility process, electrical permit, metering requirement, or site owner requires review or approval
mandatory_status.cn: China local grid or project acceptance applies domestically and is not an Oman distributor approval substitute

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] An Oman-ready EV charger package needs both product evidence and site/grid evidence, including any applicable APSR Decision 15/2023 charging-activity route. Confirm the relevant distributor or Nama connection route before quoting, shipping, or commissioning.

source.publisher: Authority for Public Services Regulation, Oman

rows.requirement: Oman EV Policy Context and Charger Rollout Signals
rows.target_requirement: Oman has public decarbonisation and energy-transition policy signals, including Oman Vision 2040 and net-zero planning, that support electrified transport and charging infrastructure development. These policy signals can influence government projects, fleet procurement, tender language, utility planning, and site-owner expectations, but they should not be treated as a stand-alone product certification or market-access approval for EV chargers.
target_standards: Oman Vision 2040 policy framework
Oman net-zero and energy-transition planning documents
Government, fleet, utility, or site-owner EV charger procurement specifications
rows.china_equivalent: China EV charger projects often reference national new-energy-vehicle policy, GB/T technical standards, domestic charging-infrastructure plans, and buyer network requirements. Chinese policy alignment does not transfer into Oman approval unless the Oman buyer or authority accepts the evidence for the specific project.
china_standards: China new-energy-vehicle and charging-infrastructure policy documents
rows.gap: Separate policy opportunity from compliance evidence. For Oman tenders, build the compliance file around the actual technical specification, connector choice, grid-connection route, safety reports, labels, manuals, data communication, warranty and maintenance obligations, and site commissioning records rather than relying only on EV policy language.

mandatory_status.target: Policy-driven or project-specific unless incorporated into a binding Oman regulation, government tender, concession, grant, fleet contract, or utility programme
mandatory_status.cn: China policy and GB/T compliance evidence is domestic context and does not by itself satisfy Oman project or authority requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Oman EV policy can create project demand, but charger acceptance still turns on the applicable DGSM, utility, tender, connector, safety, and installation requirements.
